laws.title: Public Law 22-154 | Officer Bill of Rights (OBR)
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It is a freestanding officer-rights enactment with severability, effective-date, definitions, protections, and enforcement provisions, but no express positive-law section amendment.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: freestanding law-enforcement personnel-rights rules and remedies
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a statutory note at the title-52 subject-matter anchor

current_implementation.detail: Current branch carries the Pub. L. 22-154 statutory note; the branch change is not present in origin/main.

recommended_action: Retain the statutory note at the title 52 anchor.
Do not create a new U.S. Code section.
Keep the act current.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It is a freestanding officer-rights enactment with severability, effective-date, definitions, protections, and enforcement provisions, but no express positive-law section amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 22-155 | Department of Education Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It repeals Public Law 17-129 and abolishes the Department of Education; the enactment itself is not self-repealed.

provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a statutory note at the title-20 anchor
implemented as part of the same title-20 statutory note

current_implementation.detail: Current branch includes the Pub. L. 22-155 note; this is a history-only treatment rather than a new positive-law section.

recommended_action: Retain the history-only note under the closest title-20 anchor.
Do not create a new Code section.
Preserve the repeal history for Public Law 17-129.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It repeals Public Law 17-129 and abolishes the Department of Education; the enactment itself is not self-repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 22-156 | Local Law Enforcement Agencies Reform and Nurturing (LLEARN)
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It makes direct Title 2 amendments and repeal-markings affecting the Capitol Police / Metro Police framework, and the act itself remains current.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: repeals the listed prior enactments and repeal-marked title-2 provisions
provision_level_disposition.implementation: branch already carries repeal notes for the affected title-2 material

current_implementation.detail: The current branch contains the Title 2 amendments and repeal notes; this work is absent from origin/main.

recommended_action: Keep the integrated Title 2 replacements and repeal notes.
Do not treat the act as a note-only law.
Preserve the later-law conflict handling already reflected in the current branch.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It makes direct Title 2 amendments and repeal-markings affecting the Capitol Police / Metro Police framework, and the act itself remains current.

laws.title: Public Law 22-157 | Area Management Update Act (AMUA)
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It is an amendment act directed at Public Law 19-144, not a self-repealing measure.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: amends Public Law 19-144 section 6 and section 8
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a title-18 anchor note on the current branch

current_implementation.detail: Current branch carries the Pub. L. 22-157 statutory note; no new positive-law section is required.

recommended_action: Retain the statutory note under the closest title-18 anchor.
Do not create a new Code section.
Preserve the amendment history to Public Law 19-144.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It is an amendment act directed at Public Law 19-144, not a self-repealing measure.

laws.title: Public Law 22-158 | Service Members Protection Act (SMPA)
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It is a freestanding military and employment-protection enactment with no express Code section amendment.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: service-member protections, procedural safeguards, and enforcement language
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a title-50 statutory note

current_implementation.detail: Current branch carries the Pub. L. 22-158 statutory note; no positive-law replacement was necessary.

recommended_action: Retain the title-50 statutory note.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the act current.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It is a freestanding military and employment-protection enactment with no express Code section amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 22-159 | D.C. Statute of Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: The source is retained as history only. It adds a statute-of-limitations section to Public Law 11-69 and is classified in the source record as statutory history rather than a live operative enactment.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: adds a statute-of-limitations rule to Public Law 11-69
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a history note at the title-28 anchor

current_implementation.detail: The current branch carries the history-only note, so no operative Code text should be created.

recommended_action: Retain the law only as statutory history.
Do not create or expand operative Code text.
Keep the repeal/supersession classification intact.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is retained as history only. It adds a statute-of-limitations section to Public Law 11-69 and is classified in the source record as statutory history rather than a live operative enactment.

laws.title: Public Law 22-160 | To Amend the U.S. Code
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It directly amends 18 U.S.C. 205 by replacing subsection (a) and adding subsection (j).

current_implementation.detail: Current branch contains the amended 18 U.S.C. 205 text and the Pub. L. 22-160 history note; origin/main does not.

recommended_action: Retain the updated 18 U.S.C. 205 text.
Keep the history note for Pub. L. 22-160.
No further Code change is required.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It directly amends 18 U.S.C. 205 by replacing subsection (a) and adding subsection (j).

laws.title: Public Law 22-161 | Second Chance Act
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It is a generally applicable employment-and-sealing statute with no express positive-law Code amendment.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: general employment rules and criminal-record sealing procedures
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a title-50 statutory note

current_implementation.detail: Current branch carries the Pub. L. 22-161 statutory note; no direct Code rewrite is needed.

recommended_action: Retain the statutory note under title 50.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the act current.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It is a generally applicable employment-and-sealing statute with no express positive-law Code amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 22-162 | Judicial Fairness Act
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. Section IV expressly amends 28 U.S.C. 1253, 1257, and 1291; section V amends other public laws that are outside the Code-operation scope.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: direct amendments to other public laws, not Code text

current_implementation.detail: Current branch contains the amended Title 28 text and the Pub. L. 22-162 history notes; origin/main lacks these changes.

recommended_action: Keep the integrated amendments to 28 U.S.C. 1253, 1257, and 1291.
Do not create a separate Code operation for section V.
Preserve the existing history notes.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. Section IV expressly amends 28 U.S.C. 1253, 1257, and 1291; section V amends other public laws that are outside the Code-operation scope.

laws.title: Public Law 22-163 | Employee's Bill of Rights
laws.status_basis: The source is intact and operative. It creates federal-employee protections, a District of Columbia judicial review path, and penalties, but it does not contain an express positive-law Code amendment.

provision_level_disposition.operative_character: federal-employee protections, procedure, jurisdiction, remedies, and penalties
provision_level_disposition.implementation: implemented as a title-5 statutory note

current_implementation.detail: Current branch carries the Pub. L. 22-163 statutory note; no new operative Code text is warranted.

recommended_action: Retain the title-5 statutory note.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the act current.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source is intact and operative. It creates federal-employee protections, a District of Columbia judicial review path, and penalties, but it does not contain an express positive-law Code amendment.
